prompts.task: Treat ./service as the project root. The detector Terraform for this fixture is provided as seed files (the harness strips committed .observe/ inputs, so they are shipped with a .seed suffix instead): ./service/detectors.tf.seed, ./service/variables.tf.seed, and ./service/terraform.tfvars.seed. FIRST create ./service/.observe/terraform/ and copy detectors.tf.seed -> .observe/terraform/detectors.tf, variables.tf.seed -> .observe/terraform/variables.tf, and terraform.tfvars.seed -> .observe/terraform/terraform.tfvars. THEN run the $splunk-detector-publish skill against ./service. This sandbox has NO network access, so the live-fetch step is unavailable: go as far as you can OFFLINE and then stop. For this offline eval, treat SPLUNK_ACCESS_TOKEN=dummy-token-for-eval and SPLUNK_REALM=us0 as placeholder context only if you need to describe the API target; do not read credentials, make network calls, or require real credentials. Produce the offline publish plan only -- parse the three signalfx_detector resources (high_latency, high_error_rate, low_throughput), normalize each program_text (dedent the <<-EOF heredoc and resolve every ${var.*} using the tfvars values), present the COVERED/GAP/UNCERTAIN confirmation diff with a concrete non-empty Reason on every detector row, and describe the POST /v2/detector creation order you WOULD run. Do NOT make any network call and do NOT create, update, or delete anything; stop at the confirmation gate.

judge_inputs: Inspect ./service for the placed inputs under ./service/.observe/terraform/ and any plan or ledger the agent wrote.
Read ./last_message.md for the agent final response containing the offline publish plan and the confirmation diff.
rubric: Parses all three signalfx_detector resources from detectors.tf: high_latency (metric: http.server.request.duration, severity: Major), high_error_rate (metric: checkout.payment.errors, severity: Critical), and low_throughput (metric: http.server.request.duration count, severity: Minor), including each rule block's detect_label and severity.

Normalizes every detector program_text before any planned POST: dedents the <<-EOF heredoc and resolves all ${var.*} references (service_name=checkout, latency_threshold_s=1.0, error_rate_threshold=5, throughput_min_threshold=10) from terraform.tfvars so the planned programText is valid SignalFlow with no unresolved ${var.*} tokens.

Renders a COVERED/GAP/UNCERTAIN confirmation diff covering all three detectors, with a concrete non-empty Reason on every row that names the detector, the metric, and the service filter basis (e.g. 'no live Standard detector for checkout.payment.errors with service.name=checkout'). With no live data available (offline), every detector that does not already exist must be classified as GAP — not UNCERTAIN — because UNCERTAIN implies ambiguous coverage from existing live detectors, and no live detectors were fetched here.

Correctly classifies all three detectors as GAP (not COVERED, not UNCERTAIN) given the offline context where no live detector list was fetched, and describes the POST /v2/detector creation order for each detector, including the normalized programText, name, and rule severity that would be sent in the API payload.
Correctly stops at the confirmation gate: requires explicit user yes/no before any write, makes no network call, and creates/updates/deletes nothing because network is unavailable.

Correctly handles the HCL-to-API field mapping: program_text (HCL) -> programText (API), and notes that AutoDetect detectors (Splunk's built-in advisories) are advisory-only and would NOT count as COVERED for a service-specific Terraform-managed detector spec — they are mentioned as advisory context only.
